mission.title: Install and Configure NATS
mission.description: NATS is a high-performance messaging system designed for cloud-native applications, providing a simple and secure way to communicate between services. It is suitable for microservices architectures, IoT applications, and edge computing. Key trade-offs include ease of use versus advanced features for complex messaging patterns.

steps.title: Add the NATS Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official NATS Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add nats https://charts.nats.io

helm repo update

```

> This is the official NATS Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install NATS with Helm
steps.description:
This step installs NATS using Helm, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

helm install nats nats/nats --namespace nats --create-namespace --version 2.11.0

```

> Installs NATS in the 'nats' namespace.

steps.title: Create a NATS configuration
steps.description:
This step creates a custom NATS configuration resource to define the server settings.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: nats.io/v1
kind: Nats
metadata:
  name: my-nats
  namespace: nats
spec:
  size: 3
EOF

```

> Creates a NATS cluster with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: nats.io/v1
kind: Nats
metadata:
  name: my-nats
  namespace: nats
spec:
  size: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify NATS is running
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the NATS pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n nats

```

> You should see 3 NATS pods in the 'Running' state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall NATS
uninstall.description:
This step removes the NATS installation and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall nats --namespace nats

kubectl delete namespace nats

```

> Uninstalls the NATS release.
> Deletes the NATS namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade NATS
upgrade.description:
This step includes backing up the current configuration, upgrading the NATS installation, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get nats my-nats -n nats -o yaml > nats-backup.yaml

helm upgrade nats nats/nats --namespace nats --version 2.11.1

kubectl get pods -n nats

```

> Backup the current NATS configuration.
> Upgrades NATS to the specified version.
> Verify that all NATS pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get nats my-nats -n nats -o yaml > nats-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: NATS pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration in the NATS server settings.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the NATS pods with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n nats' to identify configuration issues.

troubleshooting.title: NATS service is not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be exposed correctly or the network policy may be blocking access.

**Fix:**
Ensure the NATS service is of type 'ClusterIP' or 'LoadBalancer' and check network policies.

troubleshooting.title: Failed to create NATS configuration
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** YAML syntax errors or incorrect API version in the configuration file.

**Fix:**
Validate the YAML syntax and ensure the API version matches the installed NATS operator version.

troubleshooting.title: NATS clients cannot connect
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Firewall rules or security groups may be blocking the required ports.

**Fix:**
Ensure that ports 4222 and 8222 are open for client connections.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for JetStream API, improved monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old NATS streaming API.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.10, migrate your applications to use JetStream for message persistence.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n nats' showing all NATS pods in 'Running' state.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: nats.io/v1
kind: Nats
metadata:
  name: my-nats
  namespace: nats
spec:
  size: 3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
